text: - **Status outliving the session.** `mark_dead()` (`src/state/sessions.rs:419-428`) only flips `alive=0, working=0` locally — it does not publish a final/blank/expired kind:30315. Since kind:30315 carries a NIP-40 `expiration` set to `now + status_ttl` (`STATUS_TTL_SECS` = 90s, `domain.rs:14`) at the last heartbeat before death, peers reading the relay continue to see the dead session as "live" for up to 90s after it actually ended. Local readers are shielded from this by `derive_status()` (`src/session.rs:280-296`), which checks `Lifecycle::Active` and suppresses immediately — but that's a local-only guard; remote peers have no such signal and rely solely on relay-side expiration.
